title: Design
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5genf","text":"This protocol details the design of the workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6p3e4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1t5rr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Import hg38 reference sequence for the gene target into Benchling.
Select a target exon to determine number of SNV libraries required for coverage.
SGE oligo should not exceed 120bp.
SGE oligo should include 10-15bp of flanking intronic sequences.
For exons requiring multiple libraries, a minimum overlap of 10bp is recommended.
Design genomic Amplification primers (AMP) using Primer3.
Define your input sequence as the minimum SGE oligo and mark it as target for primer design ([target]); include 100-200bp of additional flanking sequence.
Exclude common SNPs and repeats using <exclude regions>.
Select 20bp optimal size, 60 °C optimal Tm, 160bp maximum amplicon size.
Design Linearization primers (LIN).
Generate LIN primers by reverse complementing AMP primers and adding 1-3 additional bases to the 5’ end.
Select exon coding sequence between AMP primers.
Use Benchling’s CRISPR tool to select sgRNAs.
Prioritize sites with the highest “on-target” score, and lowest “off-target” score.
Only select PAM site “NGG” where a G>C mutation in the 2nd or 3rd position would result in a synonymous mutation. It is important that this site is a minimum of 10bp from a splice site.
Use forward-strand sequence of sgRNA oligo as input for sge_plater.py to produce an IDT order form. Oligos will be complimented and adapter sequences for assembly will be added automatically.
The SGE oligo should comprise the entire sequence captured by AMP primers.
If an NGG PAM site has been selected, one other synonymous mutation should be included in the oligo. Ideally this mutation should be in a non-splicing area of the intronic region of the AMP amplicon.

If no intronic synonymous mutation is possible, introduction of a second synonymous mutation within the target coding region is necessary as an HDR marker, but coverage of other variants at this site will be lost.
Order a Twist Library including every possible single nucleotide change and every possible 3-base deletion for the given SGE oligo sequence.
creator: Nahum Smith
